## Overview

Start with the *why*. What question were you trying to answer? Who would care about this?


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
library(tidyverse)
# library(tidymodels)  # uncomment if modeling
```
:::


## The Data

Describe your data source. Where did it come from? What does each row represent?


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
# Replace with your actual data loading
# data <- read_csv("data/your-file.csv")

# Example with built-in data:
glimpse(mtcars)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
Rows: 32
Columns: 11
```

:::

## Exploration

What did you find when you first looked at the data?


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
mtcars |>
  ggplot(aes(x = wt, y = mpg, color = factor(cyl))) +
  geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.8) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("#1a3a5c", "#2e86ab", "#a8d8ea")) +
  labs(
    title = "Your Plot Title",
    x = "X Axis Label",
    y = "Y Axis Label",
    color = "Group"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13)
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![A descriptive caption for your plot](project-one_files/figure-html/eda-plot-1.png){width=672}
:::
:::


## Analysis

Walk through your methodology here. Code-fold is on by default so readers can toggle code.


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
# Your modeling code here
summary(lm(mpg ~ wt + cyl, data = mtcars))
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```

Call:
lm(formula = mpg ~ wt + cyl, data = mtcars)

Residuals:
    Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
-4.2893 -1.5512 -0.4684  1.5743  6.1004 

Coefficients:
            Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
(Intercept)  39.6863     1.7150  23.141  < 2e-16 ***
wt           -3.1910     0.7569  -4.216 0.000222 ***
cyl          -1.5078     0.4147  -3.636 0.001064 ** 
---
Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1

Residual standard error: 2.568 on 29 degrees of freedom
Multiple R-squared:  0.8302,	Adjusted R-squared:  0.8185 
F-statistic: 70.91 on 2 and 29 DF,  p-value: 6.809e-12
```


:::
:::


## Results

What did you find? Lead with the finding, then support it with the output above.

## Takeaways

- What worked well?
- What would you do differently?
- What's the next question this raises?
